what_it_does: A rigor review of the live guided experience that adopts the skeptic role, grounding every doubt in evidence marks and delivering seam-by-seam verdicts on whether the experience is achievable now.
observations: The review was not live-verified: the company bridge and the 4B brain were down, so the make-or-break comprehension test is inferred from code, not observed running.
It introduces a headline verdict: the anchor mis-locates the crux; mockup comprehension is mostly built, but the true make-or-break is the generate loop for redesign mockups.
For mockup comprehension, raw HTML is injected straight into the RHM grounding block, bypassing address_help, with a 14KB cap that the reviewed C3 mockup already exceeds.
The text streaming engine exists but is wired only to voice; the UI chat remains request/response.
Voice-in is stronger than feared: whisper.cpp is the default STT and a full live voice loop is built.
Spatial deixis ('this/here') is built, including in-mockup click capture via postMessage; temporal deixis ('that-before-this') is flagged unbuilt and hard.
The RHM driving the view is ready for live ui:// elements, with model-free tour narration, spotlight, and NEXT/BACK wiring, but in-mockup-element spotlight needs new plumbing.
The live-app generate->build->git loop is built and governed, with DENY-ALL on empty scope, operator-only approval, blast-radius at mint time, and git revert.
The mockup-side generate loop is unbuilt beyond JSONL note capture and manual lead editing; the substrate is described as retired without a built replacement.
Latency feel depends on an unbuilt text SSE and heavy per-turn prefill; VRAM/crash gotchas are operational hard-watches.
It ends with a consolidated rating table and a recommendation to treat the live and mockup loops as two distinct builds.
standouts: Headline reframing: the anchor's feared comprehension crux is actually in decent shape; the real blocker is generate for mockups.
Mockup generate is the make-or-break gap: no autonomous path edits a redesign mockup after feedback.
The live generate loop is mechanically ready and has real safety properties.
In-mockup element deixis is a built surprise; temporal deixis is the genuinely hard seam.
The 14KB mockup injection cap is already exceeded by the reviewed mockup.
conventions: Uses explicit evidence marks: Observed(file:line), Inferred, External, Anchor-idea.
Cites file:line locations for observed claims.
Distinguishes 'machinery is built' from 'I watched it work'.
Rates seams on a READY/MODERATE/HARD scale with residual-risk notes.
Honest about unbuilt parts, including to_wire flags and deferred FE wiring docstrings.
Safety-first design: empty scope deny-all, operator-only approval, blast radius at mint time, revertible git commits.
concerns: The review's own verification status undermines its strongest claims: the 4B and bridge were not running, so 'READY' means 'built and wired,' not verified.
The 14KB mockup HTML cap truncates the very mockups the surface must explain.
Mockup feedback JSONL is described as retired with no built replacement generate path surfaced.
Temporal deixis is aspirational and explicitly flagged unbuilt.
The live-app batch approve UX is inferred-unbuilt, leaving a gap between per-address intents and one-click dispatch.
VRAM/crash operational gotchas could kill live feel regardless of architecture.
notes: A skeptical, evidence-tagged readiness audit that finds the live-app loop nearly ready but declares the mockup-side generate loop the unbuilt make-or-break.
questions: What is the planned replacement for the retiring /api/mockup-feedback JSONL substrate?
Which exact 4B model is the resident brain, and what port does the bridge listen on for the listed verify test?
How is the 'batch compose-and-show' UX intended to assemble and present multiple per-address build intents for a single approval?
What does 'the lead' refer to in the mockup-feedback flow, and how does it apply notes by hand?
What HTML-to-text extraction strategy is intended if raising the 14KB cap is insufficient?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it will act as the skeptic grounding doubts in evidence, and it does so while delivering seam-level verdicts and identifying the true blocker.
apparent_themes: Live code loop versus redesign mockup loop
intention_signals: To give the anchor an honest, unflinching assessment of which parts of the guided review surface are real today, correct the mis-located crux, and scope the remaining work—especially the missing mockup-edit generate loop.
novelty: The explicit evidence-mark taxonomy and the self-aware 'not live verified' framing, plus the reframing that the feared comprehension seam is not the actual blocker.
contribution: A structured, evidence-annotated readiness audit with seam-level verdicts, residual-risk notes, a concrete verify test, and a clear statement that the mockup-side generate loop is the missing piece—useful for prioritization and verification planning.
summary_for_embedding: A rigor review of the live guided review surface that grades five seams by evidence, finds the live-app loop largely built and governed, and identifies the autonomous mockup-edit generate loop as the unbuilt make-or-break.
intention_for_embedding: To ground the anchor's hopes in observed and inferred evidence, correct the perceived crux, and point the next build at the true gap.
